cases.title: Scenario-matrix coverage for lecturer export scope restriction
preconditions: Lecturer user is authenticated and assigned to section A only.
Attendance records exist for section A (assigned) and section B (not assigned to lecturer).
Both sections belong to the same active term.
steps: Call GET /v1/reports/attendance?termId={termId} as the lecturer.
Call POST /v1/exports/attendance with format csv and filters { termId } as the lecturer.
Poll or retrieve the completed export artifact and parse CSV rows.
cases.expected: Maps to testing-plan §3.1 reporting/export and scope row (E2E-08): report rows and export output include only section A attendance data and exclude section B by default.

cases.title: Export module applies lecturer section scope at DB query boundary
preconditions: Integration database seeded with lecturer-to-section assignments and attendance rows across assigned and unassigned sections.
Export service/repository uses real DB transactions.
steps: Invoke export job creation use-case for lecturer actor with term-level filters and no explicit classSectionId.
Query persisted export job scope metadata and generated row set from repository layer.
Assert SQL-level or repository filter predicates restrict to lecturer assigned classSectionId set.
cases.expected: Module integration commits export job scoped to assigned sections only; persisted export rows and scope metadata contain zero records from unassigned sections (PRM-09 / BR-18).

cases.title: HTTP contract for scoped lecturer export job creation
preconditions: Assigned lecturer is authenticated with valid Bearer token.
Target classSectionId belongs to lecturer assignment set.
steps: Send POST /v1/exports/attendance with body { format: csv, filters: { termId, classSectionId } } and required Idempotency-Key.
Validate HTTP status code and JSON response envelope.
Validate data.exportJobId, data.status, and data.format fields per API contract.
cases.expected: Response is 202 with standard { data, meta, error } envelope; data contains exportJobId, status Queued (or equivalent async state), and format csv.

cases.title: HTTP contract for scoped lecturer attendance report query
preconditions: Assigned lecturer is authenticated.
Attendance data exists in assigned and unassigned sections for the same term.
steps: Send GET /v1/reports/attendance?termId={termId}&page=1&pageSize=25 with lecturer Authorization header.
Validate HTTP 200 and paginated response envelope.
Inspect data rows for classSectionId values.
cases.expected: Response is 200 with standard envelope and pagination meta; every returned row classSectionId is within lecturer assigned section set and excludes unassigned sections.

cases.title: Primary lecturer export workflow limited to assigned sections
preconditions: Lecturer is assigned to section SE101-01 with closed sessions containing attendance outcomes.
Another section SE101-02 in same term is taught by a different lecturer and has attendance data.
Lecturer has no assignment to SE101-02.
steps: Authenticate as lecturer assigned to SE101-01.
Request POST /v1/exports/attendance with csv format and filters covering the term.
Wait for export completion and download the CSV artifact.
Verify each CSV row section identifier matches SE101-01 only.
cases.expected: End-to-end export happy path succeeds and CSV contains only SE101-01 attendance rows with no SE101-02 or other cross-section leakage (testing-plan E2E-08).

cases.title: Lecturer denied report and export for unassigned classSectionId
preconditions: Lecturer is authenticated and assigned only to section A.
Section B exists with attendance data and is not in lecturer assignment set.
steps: Call GET /v1/reports/attendance?classSectionId={sectionBId} as lecturer.
Call POST /v1/exports/attendance with filters { classSectionId: sectionBId } as lecturer.
cases.expected: Both requests are denied with 403 Forbidden or OutOfScope semantics; responses contain no partial section B rows, counts, or export job identifiers.

cases.title: Report pagination totals reflect lecturer scope not institution-wide counts
preconditions: Institution has 120 attendance rows across multiple sections; lecturer scope covers 25 rows in one assigned section.
Integration environment uses real report query with pagination.
steps: Query GET /v1/reports/attendance with page=1 and pageSize=25 as scoped lecturer.
Read meta.pagination.totalItems and totalPages.
Advance to final page and verify row section identifiers.
cases.expected: Pagination metadata totalItems and totalPages are computed from scoped dataset (25) only; no cross-scope counts appear in meta.pagination per API §2.5.

cases.title: Term-wide export filter still excludes unassigned sections by default
preconditions: Lecturer assigned to one section in a multi-section term.
Attendance exists in assigned and multiple unassigned sections under same termId.
steps: Submit POST /v1/exports/attendance with filters { termId } only (no classSectionId).
Complete export and inspect output rows.
Attempt to include unassigned section via filter manipulation in request body.
cases.expected: Default term-wide export for lecturer includes only assigned-section rows; explicit unassigned classSectionId in filters is rejected or ignored without leaking foreign section data.

cases.title: Lecturer browser journey exports attendance from assigned sections only
preconditions: Lecturer is logged in on desktop browser.
Reporting UI shows term filter and section selector populated from lecturer scope.
Attendance data exists in assigned and unassigned sections.
steps: Navigate to attendance report or export page.
Open section filter dropdown and observe available options.
Select an assigned section, set export format CSV, and trigger export.
Confirm download or job status UI and verify exported file section scope.
cases.expected: UI section selector lists only assigned class sections; export action produces CSV scoped to selected assigned section with no unassigned section rows visible in filter or export output.
